sections.title: Overview
sections.content: A cervical collar limits flexion, extension, and lateral rotation of the cervical spine. It is used as an adjunct to manual stabilisation when spinal precautions are indicated. A collar alone does not fully immobilise the cervical spine — manual stabilisation remains essential until full immobilisation is achieved.

sections.title: Sizing & Application
sections.content:
**Sizing (Stifneck / equivalent):**

1. Measure from top of shoulder to chin with neck in neutral position

2. Match measurement to collar size markings

3. Select: no-neck (for large neck), short, regular, tall

**Application:**

1. Rescuer 1 maintains manual cervical stabilisation in neutral alignment

2. Rescuer 2 fits collar: slide posterior piece behind neck (or position front piece first per brand instructions)

3. Secure chin cup under chin; fasten velcro firmly

4. Ensure airway is not compromised and collar is not too tight

5. Maintain manual stabilisation until full immobilisation is achieved (vacuum mattress / LBB)

6. Reassess neurological status after application

sections.title: Key Points
sections.content:
- Collar does NOT replace manual immobilisation
- Never apply collar if: patient resists or has muscle spasm (force increases injury risk)
- Remove if: patient vomits, airway needs management, causes distress that worsens outcome
- Document: indications, collar size, patient tolerance and any neurological changes
